_comment: Lane direction ground truth for wrong-way detection. Each feature is a polygon region of the road, with the EXPECTED direction of legal travel for vehicles inside it. 'heading_deg' uses the math convention computed by atan2(vy, vx) in the sensor frame: 0=+X, 90=+Y, 180/-180=-X, -90=-Y. A vehicle whose velocity heading points more than 'angle_thresh_deg' (a runtime parameter) away from the lane heading is flagged as wrong-way. IMPORTANT: every feature below currently has "calibrated": false and PLACEHOLDER headings copied from a rough guess of road orientation. They MUST be measured from the real data before the WWD numbers are trustworthy (see README, section 'Calibrating lane geometry').
